title: Moroccan-Style Chickpea Stew
image_alt: Moroccan-Style Chickpea Stew served over couscous
modifications: Omitted Ras El Hanout — skipped entirely rather than substituting
Omitted Harissa — left out the heat
Added ½ cup chopped dates, half a small cauliflower, and 1½ cups seitan

items.name: spinach or kale, roughly chopped ✱ interchangeable

steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onions for 3–5 minutes until translucent. Add chickpeas, sweet potatoes, and cauliflower; cook another 5–8 minutes.
Add paprika, tomato paste, and garlic. Mix and cook 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes, broth, and dates. Lower heat to medium-low and simmer covered for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add seitan and continue simmering covered for another 10–15 minutes until the sweet potatoes and cauliflower are tender.
Add spinach (or kale) and cook until wilted. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve warm over couscous and garnish with parsley.
